steps.title: Use the given points
steps.content: The graph of $y = f(x)$ contains the points $(0, 3)$, $(9, 0)$, and $(24, 0)$. So we know directly that $f(0) = 3$, $f(9) = 0$, and $f(24) = 0$.

steps.title: Check each choice against the known values
steps.content:
• A) $f(24) < 0$: The point $(24, 0)$ tells us $f(24) = 0$, not less than zero. This cannot be true.
  • B) $f(3) < 0$: The graph passes through $(0,3)$ and $(9,0)$, so $f(3)$ is between these. We aren't told $f$ is monotonic, so $f(3)$ could be positive, negative, or zero — this could be true.
  • C) $f(3) > 0$: For the same reason, $f(3) > 0$ is possible.
  • D) $f(0) < 24$: $f(0) = 3$, and $3 < 24$, so this is true.

steps.title: Identify the answer
steps.content: Only choice A is impossible because $(24, 0)$ is on the graph, forcing $f(24) = 0$. The answer is A.
